pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py line 52 creates database 'demodb' via API call. setup_task.sh uses 'demodb' throughout. All SQL commands target demodb.
setup_task.sh line 29 inserts bot.alpha@spamnet.com. Line 37 inserts bot.beta@spamnet.com. Both emails used consistently throughout setup script.
setup_task.sh line 44 inserts profile with Email='innocent.user@normal.com'. Used in subsequent queries on lines 48 and 52.
task.json description states 'Update these Profiles by setting their AccountStatus to Suspended'. verifier.py lines 73 and 81 check if bot statuses == 'Suspended'.
task.json description states 'Create a new Vertex class SuspensionLog'. verifier.py line 58 checks has_suspension_log_class by verifying 'SuspensionLog' in classes list.
task.json description states 'Create a new Edge class HasSuspensionLog'. verifier.py line 64 checks has_edge_class by verifying 'HasSuspensionLog' in classes list.

task.json description states 'create a new SuspensionLog vertex with a property Reason set to Review Bombing'. verifier.py lines 100 and 111 check if 'Review Bombing' is in alpha_reasons and beta_reasons respectively.

privileged_info_summary: The trust_safety_reviewer_suspension task operates on a synthetic demodb database. Two bot accounts (bot.alpha@spamnet.com with 3 one-star reviews and bot.beta@spamnet.com with 4 one-star reviews) must be suspended and linked to SuspensionLog entries with reason 'Review Bombing'. An innocent user (innocent.user@normal.com with 2 one-star reviews) must not be suspended. The suspension threshold is 3 or more one-star reviews. The task requires adding an AccountStatus property to Profiles, creating SuspensionLog vertices and HasSuspensionLog edges for each suspended user.
